maml:description.maml:para: Patch a certificate.
Upload a signed certificate and/or trust chain.
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
PEM-encoded signed certificate you uploaded for your selected key type.
PEM-encoded trust chain for the signed certificate uploaded by end user.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Acknowledges any validation `warnings` present on the certificate when uploading it. The default is `false`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Acknowledges any validation `warnings` present on the certificate when uploading it. The default is `false`.
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
PEM-encoded signed certificate you uploaded for your selected key type.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
PEM-encoded trust chain for the signed certificate uploaded by end user.
List a certificate's hostname bindings.
This lists hostname bindings for the certificate identified by `certificateId`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Filter results to certificates created under the specified contract.

Returns SANs and Subject CN for certificates matching the specified domain. Matches are case-insensitive, and support wildcards. For example, `domain=example.com` returns certificates with `*.akamai.com` or `akamai.com` in the SAN list or Subject CN.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

Returns certificates that expire within the specified number of days from the request date. For example, a value of `5` returns certificates that expire in the next five days or less. A value of 0 returns only expired certificates.
Unique identifier for the group. This is an optional parameter when listing certificates.
Deployment network for the certificate, either `STAGING` or `PRODUCTION`.
Specific page from the result set, `1` by default.
Specifies the number of items to return per page. The default is `10` and the maximum is `100`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Filter results to certificates created under the specified contract.

Returns SANs and Subject CN for certificates matching the specified domain. Matches are case-insensitive, and support wildcards. For example, `domain=example.com` returns certificates with `*.akamai.com` or `akamai.com` in the SAN list or Subject CN.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

Returns certificates that expire within the specified number of days from the request date. For example, a value of `5` returns certificates that expire in the next five days or less. A value of 0 returns only expired certificates.
Unique identifier for the group. This is an optional parameter when listing certificates.
Deployment network for the certificate, either `STAGING` or `PRODUCTION`.
Specific page from the result set, `1` by default.
Specifies the number of items to return per page. The default is `10` and the maximum is `100`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Get a certificate.

List all CCM certificates, or retrieve a specific entry by its CertificateID. The response includes name, subject, SANs, and CSR for both list and individual operations, and signed certificate, plus the uploaded trust chain if present for individual certificates.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Substring match on certificate name.
Filter by the certificate's status. This parameter accepts comma-separated values that match more than one status.
Filter results to certificates created under the specified contract.

Returns SANs and Subject CN for certificates matching the specified domain. Matches are case-insensitive, and support wildcards. For example, `domain=example.com` returns certificates with `*.akamai.com` or `akamai.com` in the SAN list or Subject CN.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

Returns certificates that expire within the specified number of days from the request date. For example, a value of `5` returns certificates that expire in the next five days or less. A value of 0 returns only expired certificates.
Unique identifier for the group. This is an optional parameter when listing certificates.
If `true`, the response includes full certificate data, such as the CSR, signed certificate, and trust chain.
Substring match on the signed certificate issuer field to be able to quickly find certificates issued by certain intermediate or root CA.
Filter by key algorithm used in the certificate, either `RSA` or `ECDSA`.
Specific page from the result set, `1` by default.
Specifies the number of items to return per page. The default is `10` and the maximum is `100`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Sorts results by one or more comma-separated certificate fields, in order from left to right. Supported fields are `modifiedDate`, `expirationDate`, `createdDate`, and `certificateName`. Prefix a field with `-` for descending or `+` for ascending sort order. If you omit this parameter, results default to descending `modifiedDate`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Substring match on certificate name.
Filter by the certificate's status. This parameter accepts comma-separated values that match more than one status.
Filter results to certificates created under the specified contract.

Returns SANs and Subject CN for certificates matching the specified domain. Matches are case-insensitive, and support wildcards. For example, `domain=example.com` returns certificates with `*.akamai.com` or `akamai.com` in the SAN list or Subject CN.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

Returns certificates that expire within the specified number of days from the request date. For example, a value of `5` returns certificates that expire in the next five days or less. A value of 0 returns only expired certificates.
Unique identifier for the group. This is an optional parameter when listing certificates.
If `true`, the response includes full certificate data, such as the CSR, signed certificate, and trust chain.
Substring match on the signed certificate issuer field to be able to quickly find certificates issued by certain intermediate or root CA.
Filter by key algorithm used in the certificate, either `RSA` or `ECDSA`.
Specific page from the result set, `1` by default.
Specifies the number of items to return per page. The default is `10` and the maximum is `100`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Sorts results by one or more comma-separated certificate fields, in order from left to right. Supported fields are `modifiedDate`, `expirationDate`, `createdDate`, and `certificateName`. Prefix a field with `-` for descending or `+` for ascending sort order. If you omit this parameter, results default to descending `modifiedDate`.
Create a certificate.
This creates a third-party certificate.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Filter results to certificates created under the specified contract.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Unique identifier for the group. This is an optional parameter when listing certificates.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Substring match on certificate name.
Fully qualified domain name (FQDN) or other name associated with the subject.
Filter results to certificates created under the specified contract.
Two-letter ISO 3166 country code.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Unique identifier for the group. This is an optional parameter when listing certificates.
The key size, either `2048` for `RSA` or `P-256` for `ECDSA`.
Filter by key algorithm used in the certificate, either `RSA` or `ECDSA`.
City or locality name.
Legal name of the organization.

SAN (Subject Alternative Name) domains for the certificate. You can include up to 100 entries in this list. However, the combined total number of domains across both SANs and the subject CN mustn't exceed 100.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
TLS network compliance categories. Currently, the only allowed value is `ENHANCED_TLS`.
Full name of the state or province.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Substring match on certificate name.
Fully qualified domain name (FQDN) or other name associated with the subject.
Filter results to certificates created under the specified contract.
Two-letter ISO 3166 country code.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Unique identifier for the group. This is an optional parameter when listing certificates.
The key size, either `2048` for `RSA` or `P-256` for `ECDSA`.
Filter by key algorithm used in the certificate, either `RSA` or `ECDSA`.
City or locality name.
Legal name of the organization.

SAN (Subject Alternative Name) domains for the certificate. You can include up to 100 entries in this list. However, the combined total number of domains across both SANs and the subject CN mustn't exceed 100.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
TLS network compliance categories. Currently, the only allowed value is `ENHANCED_TLS`.
Full name of the state or province.
Delete a certificate.
This deletes a certificate. You can delete only certificates that aren't `ACTIVE`.
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Patch a certificate.
Rename a CCM certificate.
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Substring match on certificate name.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Acknowledges any validation `warnings` present on the certificate when uploading it. The default is `false`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Acknowledges any validation `warnings` present on the certificate when uploading it. The default is `false`.
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Substring match on certificate name.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Update a certificate.
This updates a certificate's data. You can rename the certificate, upload a signed certificate with an optional trust chain, or perform both actions in a single request.
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Acknowledges any validation `warnings` present on the certificate when uploading it. The default is `false`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Acknowledges any validation `warnings` present on the certificate when uploading it. The default is `false`.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Certificate identifier on which to perform the desired operation.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
